name: unified-turn-introspection-v1
description: Ground-truth assertions for the orion-unified Hub chat introspection experiment (semantic self-indexing proposal, 2026-07-11). Scoring is lexical: an assertion passes when any of its any_of substrings appears (case-insensitive) in the answer. Evidence entries anchor each assertion to a repo file and a literal symbol the gate test verifies still exists.

questions.prompt: Trace an Orion-mode Hub WebSocket chat message from receipt through stance/thought assembly, FCC motor execution, finalization, the WebSocket response, and chat-history persistence. Name the concrete functions and services that own each phase.
Where is the FCC motor prompt for a unified Hub turn assembled, and which stance, grounding, autonomy, repair, user, and tool inputs can enter it?
In the unified Hub turn, what is the difference between the FCC motor draft, HarnessDraftMoleculeV1, HarnessRunV1.final_text, and the final Hub WebSocket frame? Which component produces each?
If the Thought service returns a defer or refuse disposition for a unified Hub turn, which later phases are skipped and what does Hub emit to the client?
The FCC motor produced a draft, but the user received a finalize-phase error. Where should an investigator begin, and which partial artifacts should still exist?
Which stage of the unified turn can change the FCC motor draft before it reaches Juniper, and how is that change reported?
How does Hub know a long-running harness-governor request is still alive during a unified turn, and what causes a harness_rpc_timeout?
In the unified Hub turn, which outputs are persisted only after a successful final handoff to the client, and which governor artifacts are emitted even when later phases fail?
Which important edges in the unified Hub turn saga are bus- or plan-mediated and therefore likely to be missing or incomplete in a static call graph of this repository?

route_gate.summary: Identifies the Orion-mode route gate in websocket_handler.py

hub_orchestration_boundary.summary: Names execute_unified_turn() as the Hub-side saga boundary

pre_thought_context.summary: Mentions pre-turn appraisal / association bundle construction before Thought

defer_refuse_pre_motor.summary: Recognizes defer/refuse disposition as a pre-motor terminal path

hub_governor_handoff.summary: Names HarnessRunRequestV1 as the Hub-to-governor bus RPC handoff

step_relay_liveness.summary: Explains step relay as WebSocket progress and governor liveness evidence

motor_context_assembly.summary: Names compile_harness_prefix() as the motor-context assembly point

motor_draft_path.summary: Names HarnessRunner.run()/run_fcc_turn() as the draft-producing motor path

motor_evidence.summary: Cites grammar receipts and the draft molecule as motor evidence

finalize_beats.summary: Distinguishes substrate appraisal, reflection/quick gate, and voice finalization as finalize beats

draft_not_final.summary: Names finalize_changed as the draft-to-final difference indicator

voice_finalize_failure.summary: Starts a finalize-phase failure investigation at the voice finalize stage

run_result_boundary.summary: Names HarnessRunV1 / the run artifact as the governor-to-Hub result boundary

persistence_after_handoff.summary: Identifies post-success chat-history/turn/Spark persistence in Hub

graph_limitations.summary: Recognizes bus RPC and dynamic plan edges as static-graph blind spots
